title: Toolsmith: Pi writes its own ToolSpec modules under the verify gate
component: prompts/consciousness.txt (procedure), docs/adr (new ADR), agent/tools.py registry (unchanged — that's the point)
current_state: Adding a tool is one TOOLS=[...] entry in one module (ADR-002), Pi has file-edit tools, and after T-181/T-182 it can test its own edits safely. Yet 'Pi, add yourself a tool' has no defined procedure — it would improvise.
target_state: A documented, prompt-known TOOLSMITH procedure: ticket first (create_ticket) → write tools/tools_<name>.py with TOOLS export + handler + tests in testing/test_tools_<name>.py → run_tests scoped → run_verify full → append solution → tell Ash to review the branch. Registered in consciousness as the ONLY way Pi adds capabilities. Registry auto-discovers the module once added to _TOOL_MODULES (the single manual line, called out in the procedure). First supervised run: Ash picks a small real tool want and watches Pi execute the procedure end-to-end.
migration_plan: 1. ADR-007: self-extension procedure + boundaries (no new deps without Ash; no god-path; no network-credential tools).
2. Consciousness gains the procedure (short — it references the ADR via include, not inline prose).
3. Template: docs/templates/tool_module_template.py with ToolSpec skeleton + test skeleton.
4. Supervised first run on a real small tool; its solution entry becomes the worked example.
5. Test: template module passes registry duplicate/alias asserts when instantiated under a temp name.
risk_notes: The gate chain (ticket→test→verify→solution) is what makes this safe. Boundaries in the ADR are hard rules, not suggestions — especially no-new-dependencies and no-credential-handling without explicit Ash approval.
